_meta.purpose: Solver correctness validation fixtures — competitive decklists for benchmarking combo finding against known meta archetypes

ddd-wcq-top4.source: ygoprodeck.com — D/D/D, Top 4 Cali WCQ Regional (John Ramírez Barberi, 2025-12-07)

ryzeal-mitsurugi-hk-top8.source: ygoprodeck.com — Mitsurugi Ryzeal, Top 8 Yu-Gi-Oh! Open Tournament Hong Kong 2026 AE (Tu Kai-Hsiang, 2026-03-23)

branded-dracotail-bainbridge-2nd.source: ygoprodeck.com — Branded Dracotail, 2nd Place Bainbridge WCQ Regional

nekroz-ryzeal-jan2026-tcg-1st.source: ygoprodeck.com — 1st Place Undefeated Nekroz Ryzeal (Jan. 2026 TCG Format)
nekroz-ryzeal-jan2026-tcg-1st.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 1. Ritual archetype fixture — validates F1 Ritual Unlock Co-Presence generalization beyond Mitsurugi Prayers. Nekroz uses distinct ritual spells (Kaleidoscope, Cycle, Mirror) and distinct ritual monsters (Brionac, Trishula, Unicore, Areadbhair). Ryzeal engine co-presence is incidental (hybrid tournament build); F1 signal isolates on Nekroz-specific ritual metadata. Aliases normalized: Bagooska 90590304→90590303, Harpie's Feather Duster 18144507→18144506.

labrynth-2026.source: ygoprodeck.com — Labrynth 2026 (competitive Jan 2026 build with Dogmatika engine)
labrynth-2026.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 2. Control paradigm fixture (trap-heavy Fiend archetype with Dogmatika sub-engine). Diversifies control representation beyond Radiant Typhoon. Aliases normalized: Droll & Lock Bird 94145022→94145021. Note: the modern Fydraulis/Solemn Report/Dominus Spark variant (labrynth-jan-2026-v2-685973) was rejected as those cards are absent from cards.cdb; this older build trades none of its control identity.

stun-runick-melbourne-top8.source: ygoprodeck.com — Stun Runick (Melbourne WCQ Regional Top 8)
stun-runick-melbourne-top8.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 2. Stall paradigm fixture — pure Runick (no Sky Striker / Paleo hybrid) with floodgate suite (Rivalry of Warlords, There Can Be Only One, Gozen Match, Synchro Zone). Tests the solver on a non-summoning engine: wins via banishing opponent's deck through Runick quick-play spells. F1/F2/F3 structural value function should mostly NOT fire (no rituals, no tutor chain, no extra-deck material pool beyond Hugin/Munin Link-1 wings). Useful negative control for step 3 ES tuning — confirms structural weights don't hallucinate bonus on an engine-less deck.

snake-eye-yummy-sarcophagus-hollywood-wc.source: ygoprodeck.com — Shining Sarcophagus Fiendsmith Snake-Eye Yummy (Hollywood WCQ Regional winner, Jad Andary, 2026-03-22)
snake-eye-yummy-sarcophagus-hollywood-wc.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 3. Iconic 2024-2026 meta combo archetype — Snake-Eye engine with Fiendsmith + Yummy + Shining Sarcophagus sub-engines (multi-engine hybrid is the post-banlist canonical form). High combo density stresses F2 (tutor chain) + F3 (Extra Deck material pool). Aliases normalized: Ash Blossom 14558128→14558127, Harpie's Feather Duster 18144507→18144506.

tearlaments-2026-fafnirymd.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 3. GY-fusion paradigm — Tearlaments win via discard-to-mill that fuses Extra Deck monsters from GY. Distinct from Branded's in-hand ritual-like fusion pattern. Tests F3 Extra Deck Material Pool on mill-driven fusion summons (Kitkallos, Rulkallos, Kaleido-Heart). No side deck (list was published without side). No alias normalization needed.

floowandereeze-2026.source: ygoprodeck.com — FLOOWANDEREEZE - yes floo is still good without feather storm (2026 build)
floowandereeze-2026.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 3. Normal-summon lock paradigm — Floowandereeze banish-normal-summon cards from hand to chain into Robina/Eglen/Toccan searches and end on Empen (spell/trap-activation lock) + Mist Valley Apex Avian (negate). Tests the solver on non-standard summoning (no Special Summon during turn-1 for main plays, all normal-summon via banish effect). Very small side deck (6 cards). No alias normalization needed.

kashtira-azamina-verquin-wcq-top8.source: ygoprodeck.com — Azamina Kashtira Radiant Typhoon Maliss (Verquin WCQ Regional Top 8, Edouard Tan, 2026-04-04)
kashtira-azamina-verquin-wcq-top8.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 4. Kashtira banish-setup engine fused with Maliss (new 2025-2026 archetype using 'P'/'C'/'Q' indirection cards) + Radiant Typhoon + Azamina Fusion sub-engines. Multi-engine meta hybrid — stresses F2 tutor chain across 4 tutorable engines. Aliases normalized: Monster Reborn 83764719→83764718. Note: Radiant Typhoon engine co-presence means F3 scoring signal partially overlaps with radiant-typhoon-opener fixture — evaluate delta against Kashtira-specific endboard pieces only.

horus-crystron-santiago-wcq-top4.source: ygoprodeck.com — Horus Crystron (Santiago WCQ Regional Top 4, Guillermo Enrique Rivera Gonzalez, 2026-03-21)
horus-crystron-santiago-wcq-top4.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 4. Synchro paradigm — Crystron engine (tuner + non-tuner zombies recurring from GY) climbing into Rank/Link chains with Horus king engine (Imsety/Hapi) via Sarcophagus loop. Cleanly isolates synchro climbing (no other fixture has a synchro primary engine). Aliases normalized: Ash Blossom 14558128→14558127, Foolish Burial 81439174→81439173.

dinomorphia-696904.source: ygoprodeck.com — When the dinos are dividing their LP (Dinomorphia Solemn Anti, 2026-03-03)
dinomorphia-696904.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 4. Unique LP-sacrifice rogue paradigm — Dinomorphia pays 50% LP to activate effects, stabilizing at low LP where opponent can't burn out. Different resource-management paradigm (LP as currency) — tests solver robustness to non-standard cost structures. No alias normalization needed.

spright-greenville-wcq-top8.source: ygoprodeck.com — Spright (Greenville WCQ Regional Top 8, Ivan Villa, 2026-01-10)
spright-greenville-wcq-top8.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for step 2 Phase 4. Level/Rank 2 toolbox paradigm — Spright Blue searches Level 2 monsters, chain Xyz into Gigantic Spright / Toadally Awesome / Number 65. Rogue archetype. Frog extenders (Swap Frog) + Nimble extenders add summoning depth. Aliases normalized: Ash Blossom 14558128→14558127, Foolish Burial 81439174→81439173, Harpie's Feather Duster 18144507→18144506.

radiant-typhoon-ygoprodeck-2026.notes: Control deck paradigm — coverage of non-combo endboard (3 cards vs typical 5-6). Opener + endboard validated by user 2026-04-15.

_meta.usage: Solver correctness validation. Decklists extracted from ygoprodeck.com HTML (data-card attributes) and validated against duel-server/data/cards.cdb. Alias IDs normalized to canonical IDs (Ash Blossom 14558128→14558127, Harpie's Feather Duster 18144507→18144506). Feed hands to the DFS/MCTS solver and compare computed endBoardCards against expected boards.
_meta.expectedBoards: Populated per hand with human-confirmed target boards. Each hand entry has an 'expectedBoard' field listing { zone, cardId, cardName } entries that the solver should reproduce. These are curated, not auto-generated — treat as golden references.

hands.description: D/D/D Pendulum opener: Savant Kepler + Savant Copernicus + Scale Surveyor + Dark Contract with the Gate + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Canonical 1-card Gate combo produces the FULL endboard: Deus Machinex+1 contract, Wave High King Caesar, Cursed King Siegfried, Sky King Zeus Ragnarok, Headhunt. See ddd-combo-reference.md.

Mitsurugi+Ryzeal opener (HK Top 8 Mar 2026, Tu Kai-Hsiang): Ame no Habakiri no Mitsurugi + Ice Ryzeal + Sword Ryzeal + Mitsurugi Prayers + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [mitsurugi-ryzeal-combo-reference.md](mitsurugi-ryzeal-combo-reference.md). Hand and endboard kept as-is from pre-existing fixture (already user-validated in step 1 calibration cycle). Realistic hybrid 2-engine 1+2-card opener: Habakiri starter + 2 Ryzeal extenders + Mitsurugi Prayers ritual spell + 1 handtrap. Canonical 6-piece endboard (Rank 4 Detonator + Rank 4 Duo Drive + Futsu no Mitama ritual + Rank 8 Photon Lord + Ryzeal Cross set + Great Purification trap set) matches research-documented Hong Kong Top 8 finish pattern. Structural sanity checks pass: no consumed-piece conflicts, no intermediate set traps, no side-deck contamination. Step 1 structural value function delta: score 35 → 42.58 (+7.58 from F1 Ritual + F2 Tutor + F3 ED Pool features).
hands._expectedBoardNote: Revised 2026-04-18: removed Number 90 Galaxy-Eyes Photon Lord (8165596). Combo analysis showed Photon Lord (Rank 8, needs 2 Lv8 simultaneously) is not reachable from this hand without 3+ ritual-summon events, which exceeds the deck's available resources (2 ritual spells × 2 effects each / turn, but search bottlenecks limit effective RS to 2). Target reduced to 5/5 canonical board (Ryzeal Detonator + Ryzeal Duo Drive + Futsu + Cross set + Purification set).

hands.description: Radiant Typhoon opener (validated 2026-04-15, re-validated 2026-04-17): Eldam + Chant + Krosea + Vision + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Ground-truthed against [radiant-typhoon-combo-reference.md](radiant-typhoon-combo-reference.md). Hand and expectedBoard KEPT from user's original validation — all 3 pieces match the canonical 2026 Radiant Typhoon control endboard (Totem Bird Rank 3 Xyz + Hraesvelgr Link-3 + Mandate Continuous Trap). Control deck paradigm — 3-card endboard (vs 5-6 for combo decks). Control paradigm gold standard for step 3 ES tuning — 3-piece endboard demonstrates high per-card disruption value vs combo's wider pile. Methodology v2 position fields already applied (attack/attack/set).

Branded Dracotail opener (Arthalion line, user-audited 2026-04-13, re-validated 2026-04-17): Dracotail Faimena + Dracotail Lukias + Mululu + Branded Fusion + Blazing Cartesia. Ground-truthed against [branded-dracotail-combo-reference.md](branded-dracotail-combo-reference.md). Hand and expectedBoard KEPT from prior user audit — all 6 pieces (Arthalion + Ecclesia + 3 Dracotail trap set + Faimena kept in HAND) match the canonical Fusion+Dracotail-trap-package turn-1 finish. Pan was swapped out for Fallen of the White Dragon in the 2026-04-13 fixture audit (Pan required tribute, dead in opening hands) — this correction is preserved. Multi-line fixture: pairs with branded-dracotail-opener-mirrorjade-line which tests the alternate Branded-centric endboard from the same opener. Methodology v2 position fields already applied.

Kashtira Azamina Radiant Typhoon Maliss opener (Verquin WCQ Top 8 Apr 2026, Edouard Tan): Kashtira Fenrir + Pressured Planet Wraitsoth + WANTED + Diabellstar + Magicians' Souls. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [kashtira-azamina-combo-reference.md](kashtira-azamina-combo-reference.md). Multi-engine Verquin hand — Kashtira engine primed (Fenrir + Wraitsoth), Azamina pivot primed (WANTED + Diabellstar), Magicians' Souls for +2 draw ramp. This deck intentionally carries no handtraps in main — the realistic Verquin opener is 5 engine cards. Canonical endboard for this hybrid: Azamina Ilia Silvia (primary omni-negate Fusion) + Accesscode Talker (Link-4 finisher) + Mecha Phantom Beast Dracossack (Rank 7 Xyz via 2x Kashtira Lvl 7) + WANTED trap set. Kashtira Arise-Heart/Shangri-Ira are NOT in this deck's extra — this is NOT a pure Kashtira build; the engine uses Kashtira bodies as XYZ material for Dracossack instead of Kashtira Rank 7 Xyz bosses.

Horus Crystron opener (Santiago WCQ Top 4 Mar 2026, Guillermo Enrique Rivera Gonzalez): Crystron Sulfefnir + Imsety Glory of Horus + King's Sarcophagus + Ash Blossom + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [horus-crystron-combo-reference.md](horus-crystron-combo-reference.md). Hand corrected: previous version had both Sulfefnir AND Sulfador in hand (over-loaded Crystron engine), new version uses single Sulfefnir starter that chains into Sulfador from deck via Inclusion. Only fixture in suite with a primary synchro engine — preserves synchro paradigm coverage for step 3. Corrected expectedBoard: Eleskeletus removed (Link-2 consumed as Crystron Synchro material, not terminal); Cyber Dragon Infinity added as Rank 8 terminal via Imsety + Hapi Xyz chain. Note: Infinity + Imsety co-presence flagged in reference doc — if smoke reveals structural infeasibility, swap Imsety for Eleskeletus.

Dinomorphia opener (Mar 2026 'When the dinos are dividing their LP' build): Dinomorphia Therizia + Dinomorphia Frenzy + Card of Demise + Pot of Duality + Iron Thunder. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [dinomorphia-combo-reference.md](dinomorphia-combo-reference.md). LP-sacrifice paradigm — compact 5-card opener where Therizia starts the fusion chain and Card of Demise provides +3 turn-1 card advantage. Canonical endboard is MINIMALIST by design (Rexterm alone + 2 set traps, NOT 2 fusion bosses simultaneously). Kentregina is CONSUMED as Rexterm's fusion material in the canonical chain, so listing both was structurally wrong in the previous fixture.

Spright opener (Greenville WCQ Top 8 Jan 2026, Ivan Villa): Spright Blue + Swap Frog + Spright Starter + Ash Blossom + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [spright-combo-reference.md](spright-combo-reference.md). Hand corrected from 4-Spright-starters + Nimble (over-loaded) to 1 Spright starter + 1 Frog extender + 1 engine spell + 2 handtraps (realistic tournament hand). Swap Frog is the dedicated Lvl 2 Aqua extender required to reach Toadally Awesome (Rank 2 WATER Aqua Xyz). Level/Rank 2 toolbox paradigm: Blue searches Starter → Starter SS Jet from deck → Blue+Jet Rank 2 into Gigantic → Gigantic SS Swap Frog → Swap Frog + Jet into Toadally. Expected endboard kept identical to pre-validation (already canonically correct): Spright Red + Carrot + Gigantic + Toadally, dual-negate + toolbox setup.

Snake-Eye Yummy Sarcophagus opener (Hollywood WCQ winner Mar 2026, Jad Andary): Snake-Eye Ash + Ash Blossom + Mulcharmy Fuwalos + Fabled Lurrie + Fiendsmith Engraver. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [snake-eye-combo-reference.md](snake-eye-combo-reference.md). Pure 1-card Snake-Eye Ash opener with 2 handtraps + Fiendsmith engine support (Fabled Lurrie discard fodder + Engraver in hand for deterministic engine activation). Expected endboard (reachable from THIS deck's extra): Flamberge Dragon (Snake-Eye boss, continuous-spell revival loop) + Fiendsmith's Desirae (Fusion omni-negate via Lacrima) + S:P Little Knight (Link-2 banish) + Snake-Eyes Doomed Dragon (Fusion finisher on 2x Lvl 1 FIRE). I:P Masquerena excluded — side-deck only, not extra. Promethean Princess excluded — not in this deck.

Tearlaments opener (Feb 2026 fafnirymd build): Tearlaments Reinoheart + Tearlaments Scheiren + Fabled Lurrie + Fiendsmith Engraver + Mulcharmy Fuwalos. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [tearlaments-combo-reference.md](tearlaments-combo-reference.md). Hand corrected from 4-Tearlaments-over-loaded to 2-Tearlaments-core + Fiendsmith engine + handtrap (realistic tournament hand). GY-fusion paradigm: Reinoheart mills Scheiren → Scheiren SS + mills 3 → multiple Tearlaments in GY → Fusion chain into Kitkallos → second Fusion into Kaleido-Heart via Scream recycle → Fiendsmith engine via Engraver+Lurrie discards. Corrected expectedBoard: Kitkallos + Kaleido-Heart + Fiendsmith's Requiem + S:P Little Knight. Rulkallos removed (typically consumed as Kaleido-Heart material, not terminal). Scream set removed (it's a field spell active in FIELD zone not set trap). Paradigm caveat for step 3: GY-fusion shows high az% because engine self-terminates quickly once Fusions land — worth cross-paradigm scoring normalization research.

Floowandereeze opener (2026 build): Floowandereeze & Robina + Floowandereeze and the Magnificent Map + Floowandereeze & Eglen + Floowandereeze & Toccan + Ghost Belle. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [floowandereeze-combo-reference.md](floowandereeze-combo-reference.md). Hand and endboard KEPT — already match canonical 1-card Robina opener + Empen/Apex Avian/Nightingale/Map end pattern. Normal-summon lock paradigm: banish cards from hand as tribute to normal-summon Flo Lvl 1 birds, chain searches, Tribute Summon Lvl 8/10 finishers. Position fields (methodology v2): Empen + Apex Avian + Nightingale in attack (ATK-position is canonical for offensive boss placement); Map has no position (field spell zone).

Labrynth opener (Jan 2026 TCG): Labrynth Stovie Torbie + Welcome Labrynth + Arias the Labrynth Butler + Arianna the Labrynth Servant + Infinite Impermanence. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [labrynth-combo-reference.md](labrynth-combo-reference.md). Hand KEPT as-is after probe experiment (2026-04-17): attempted realism refinement to [Stovie + 2 engine traps + Rollback + Imperm] dropped score 8→6, depth 31→17, matched 2/4→0/4 (t2 jumped 40.5%→77.3% indicating engine-stall). Diagnosis: Labrynth's Big Welcome Labrynth trap has a bounce-SS clause that RE-TRIGGERS Labrynth monster effects when owned monsters are bounced. Multiple Labrynth monsters in hand (Arias + Arianna + Stovie) form a CHAIN of triggers when bounced by Big Welcome, NOT redundancy. The original 3-Labrynth-monster hand is the realistic engine-dense opener. Endboard kept: Lady Labrynth + 3 set traps (Welcome + Big Welcome + Transaction Rollback).

expectedBoard.cardName: Lady Labrynth of the Silver Castle

hands.description: Stun Runick opener (Melbourne WCQ Top 8): Runick Fountain + Runick Destruction + Runick Flashing Fire + Runick Tip + Pot of Duality. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [stun-runick-combo-reference.md](stun-runick-combo-reference.md). Hand KEPT as-is after validation probe — this is a realistic Stun Runick engine-heavy opener (3 Runick Quick-Plays enable multiple Link-1 SS + Fountain recycling engine). Experimentally validated: swapping out Runick Tip (31562086) drops structural score 12→0, confirming Tip's F2 tutor-chain contribution is critical to the Runick engine's scoring footprint. Stall/non-summoning paradigm: no Ritual/Fusion/Synchro/Xyz/Link bosses beyond Link-1 Runick Wings. Turn-1 goal: Fountain active + Runick Quick-Play SS multiple Link-1s + Runick spells set for opp-turn. Extended expectedBoard to 4 pieces (added Rivalry of Warlords as realistic floodgate if drawn from deck via engine). CRITICAL caveat for step 3 ES tuning: structural score is intentionally LOW (F1=0, F2 = Tip chain only, F3 = Link-1 Wings only) — this is paradigm-correct floor, not solver weakness.

Nekroz Ryzeal opener (Jan 2026 TCG 1st Place Undefeated): Emilia (Dance Priestess) + Avance (Swordsman) + Preparation of Rites + Mulcharmy Fuwalos + Ash Blossom. Ground-truthed 2026-04-17 against [nekroz-ryzeal-combo-reference.md](nekroz-ryzeal-combo-reference.md). Hand matches the canonical 2026 Nekroz Ryzeal core 2-card combo per YGOrganization CDP (Emilia + Avance = Level 4 Nekroz pair). Corrected expectedBoard: Herald of the Arc Light REMOVED (it's consumed as Kaleidoscope's Extra-Deck ritual tribute → GY, not on-field at end). Added Nekroz of Trishula (terminal ritual boss with 3-card banish) + Ryzeal Detonator (terminal Rank 4 finisher) in place. Fixture was originally added in step 2 Phase 1 to validate F1 Ritual Unlock Co-Presence generalization on Nekroz's distinct ritual spells (Kaleidoscope/Cycle/Mirror/Divinemirror) — F1 generalization confirmed via score=46.58 pre-validation smoke.

Branded Dracotail opener (Mirrorjade line, user-added 2026-04-14, re-validated 2026-04-17): SAME opening hand as branded-dracotail-opener. Ground-truthed against [branded-dracotail-combo-reference.md](branded-dracotail-combo-reference.md). Tests the alternate canonical Branded-primary endboard: Branded Fusion → Lubellion → Mirrorjade + Albion Sanctifire atop, with Dracotail trap package SZONE + Faimena kept HAND. Multi-line fixture pairing: `matched` on EITHER this fixture OR branded-dracotail-opener indicates canonical reach — the solver explores both lines from the same opener via Branded Fusion's split targets. Methodology v2 position fields already applied.
